codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the My profile page. The page correctly displays all required user data (avatar, email, VIP badge, invite code, credit points), a 3-column balance dashboard with properly formatted currency, 8 menu items with colored icons and chevron arrows linking to sub-pages, and a logout button. The API endpoint at GET /api/user/profile properly authenticates via getServerSession, queries Prisma with explicit field selection (excluding password), and handles 401/404/500 error cases. Tests are comprehensive with 4 API tests and 9 component tests covering success, auth failure, not found, security (no password leak), loading state, error handling, and menu navigation. A few minor non-blocking observations below.

issues.description: When fetch returns a non-ok response (e.g., 500), the code silently falls through without setting an error state — profile stays null and 'Failed to load profile' is shown, but the error isn't logged. Consider adding an explicit check like `if (!response.ok) throw new Error(...)` so the catch block captures API errors too, not just network failures.

Uses raw tag instead of Next.js component for avatar rendering. Other pages in the codebase (football, basketball, concert, events) use next/image. This is consistent with some existing pages that also use and the handoff notes 'pre-existing img tag warnings', but ideally should use next/image for optimization.

The useEffect depends on `session` object reference. Since useSession returns a new object on each render, this could cause unnecessary re-fetches in certain scenarios. Using `session?.user?.id` or `status` as dependency would be more stable. In practice this works because useSession stabilizes the reference when status is 'authenticated', but it's worth noting.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture.md library doc still states 'the app does NOT wrap the layout in a NextAuth SessionProvider' under Auth Patterns, but SessionProvider was added to LayoutWrapper in commit 58233a7 (before this feature). Multiple pages now rely on useSession(). This documentation is stale and could mislead future workers into thinking SessionProvider needs to be added.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: components/LayoutWrapper.tsx:4 imports SessionProvider, wraps children at line 17-20. .factory/library/architecture.md Auth Patterns section says 'the app does NOT wrap the layout in a NextAuth SessionProvider'. At least 5 pages now use useSession: login, my/page, my/orders, my/vip, my/personal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No consistent pattern for whether pages use next/image or raw . Some pages (football, basketball, concert, events/[id]) use next/image, while this profile page and potentially others use raw . AGENTS.md doesn't specify which to use.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/my/page.tsx:116 uses for avatar. app/football/page.tsx, app/basketball/page.tsx, app/concert/page.tsx, app/events/[id]/page.tsx use next/image. Handoff notes 'pre-existing img tag warnings for other components'.

summary: The my-profile-page feature is well-implemented and passes review. All required elements are present: blue gradient header with user info, balance dashboard with proper currency formatting, 8 menu items with icons and navigation, and a functional logout button. The API endpoint correctly handles authentication, excludes the password field, and returns appropriate error codes. Test coverage is comprehensive (13 tests across API and component). Three non-blocking issues were identified: silent error handling for non-ok API responses, use of raw instead of next/image, and a minor useEffect dependency concern. Two shared state observations noted: stale architecture.md documentation about SessionProvider, and missing convention guidance about next/image vs usage.
